active_ingredients: Doxylamine succinate 25 mg tablets (nighttime sleep aid, e.g., Unisom SleepTabs); also the antihistamine in many 'PM' pain products and, with pyridoxine/vitamin B6, in a prescription product for nausea of pregnancy
indications_and_usage: Uses: as a nighttime sleep aid, for the relief of occasional sleeplessness; it helps you fall asleep if you are having trouble. It is a sedating (first-generation) antihistamine. (In combination with pyridoxine it is also prescribed for nausea and vomiting of pregnancy that has not responded to conservative measures, under a doctor's care.)
contraindications: Do not use in children under 12 years; do not use if you have a breathing problem such as emphysema or chronic bronchitis, glaucoma, or difficulty urinating due to an enlarged prostate, unless directed by a doctor; do not use with any other product containing an antihistamine, including those applied to the skin; hypersensitivity to doxylamine.
warnings_and_cautions: May cause marked drowsiness - avoid alcohol, sedatives, and tranquilizers, and do not drive or operate machinery; next-morning grogginess can occur. Anticholinergic effects (dry mouth, blurred vision, constipation, urinary retention, confusion) - older adults are especially sensitive and should generally avoid it (Beers list). Sleeplessness that lasts more than 2 weeks may be a sign of a serious underlying medical illness - see a doctor; do not use this product for more than 2 weeks. Stop use and ask a doctor if sleeplessness persists continuously for more than 2 weeks.
adverse_reactions: Drowsiness, dry mouth/nose/throat, dizziness, blurred vision, constipation, urinary retention; next-day sedation; paradoxical excitement in some people, especially children and the elderly.
drug_interactions: Alcohol, benzodiazepines, opioids, other sleep aids, and other CNS depressants (additive sedation/respiratory depression); MAO inhibitors (prolonged and intensified anticholinergic effects); other anticholinergic drugs (additive effects).
dosage_and_administration: Adults and children 12 years and over: take 25 mg (1 tablet) by mouth 30 minutes before going to bed; take only once per 24 hours, only at bedtime; if drowsiness is excessive the next day, this medicine may not be right for you. Do not use for more than 2 weeks unless directed by a doctor. Children under 12: do not use. (For nausea of pregnancy, only use the doctor-prescribed doxylamine-pyridoxine product, at the prescribed dose.)
overdosage: Overdose causes severe anticholinergic toxicity (dry, flushed skin, dilated pupils, fever, fast heartbeat, agitation, hallucinations, urinary retention), CNS depression or excitation, seizures, and, in large overdoses, rhabdomyolysis, wide-complex arrhythmias, and coma - dangerous especially in children. Seek emergency care; call Poison Control (1-800-222-1222) immediately.
